pr.title: Non-record: Depth Recurrence in Parameter-Constrained Transformers — What Works, What Doesn't, and Why

output.summary: Depth-recurrent Middle-Cycle looped transformers with noisy QAT reveal recurrence hurts due to quantization compounding and step overhead; 3x3 loops beat 2x5.
techniques: Noisy QAT matching int8 error on shared loop blocks
Middle-Cycle stem-core-tail layout emphasizing more unique blocks per loop
Controlled flat vs looped ablations to expose recurrence costs
